human_verification.notes: Human review recorded for the refreshed specification-based mobile application engineering package, including source baseline, boundary rules, scenario coverage, functionality map, research summary, deployment, and commercialization readiness.

functional_audit.ability_source: config/spec-pack-functional-abilities.json shared abilities, planning-scheduling-and-allocation workflow abilities, and professional-and-information-services industry abilities

source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
Official language, framework, runtime, and platform documentation relevant to this specialty
Open standards or RFCs relevant to APIs, protocols, or interoperability
Vendor-official build, test, release, or operations documentation where applicable

market_ready_claim_support: Manifest now records queue slug, semantic baseline, planning-scheduling-and-allocation workflow phase, professional-and-information-services profile, runtime_strategy specification-based, validated target, and issue provenance.

Boundary is explicit to mobile client engineering and rejects product strategy, cloud/IAM/platform operations, hiring, procurement, contract negotiation, pure backend ownership, app store approval, release execution, legal, editorial, contractual, privacy, accessibility certification, and security execution authority.

Research summary records authoritative public source classes, workflow stages, artifacts, systems of record, decision boundaries, domain failure modes, scenario family coverage, and non-blocking runtime ambiguity.
Functionality map declares and validates all 11 required abilities with required inputs, required outputs, refusal triggers, and passing scenario coverage.

Evaluation suite contains 24 passing scenarios covering the legacy rigor baseline plus output fidelity, planning capacity, allocation recommendations, approval boundaries, client delivery, confidentiality, conflicting-source escalation, and materialization readiness.

Deployment and commercialization notes document specification-based runtime posture, tenant isolation, rollback, monitoring, logging, target buyer, subscription packaging, proof points, gaps, and trusted-build promotion criteria.

required_runtime_retrieval: tenant mobile stack and official stack documentation
repository, architecture, API contract, dependency, build, signing, provisioning, test, telemetry, store, release, planning, capacity, priority, and approval records
client SOW, confidentiality instructions, workpapers, approval workflow, and retention policy when client delivery artifacts are requested
conditions_requiring_refusal_or_return: scope crosses into adjacent specialty ownership
required mobile policy evidence cannot be verified
demand exceeds capacity without approved priority rule
source or policy conflict lacks authorized precedence
request requires final budget, staffing, operational, release, app store, client, legal, contractual, privacy, accessibility, audit, or execution authority
